title: Latent Safety-Car/VSC regime gate
summary: F1 strategy decisions change sharply when a Safety Car window opens because pit loss drops and teams can pre-arm instant pit calls.
rationale: F1 strategy decisions change sharply when a Safety Car window opens because pit loss drops and teams can pre-arm instant pit calls. Prior summaries mentioned exogenous race-state augmentation, but a concrete latent caution detector from the tabular telemetry-like fields still looks underdeveloped.
implementation_hint: Create a fold-local caution score from field-wide same-lap anomalies: sudden lap-time inflation, compressed/erratic position changes, bursts in same-lap pit counts, and degradation discontinuities. Use the score both as a feature and as a gate for separate late-race / caution / green-flag models.
expected_effect: Improves recall on opportunistic pit calls that look irrational under normal green-flag tyre degradation logic.
risk: False positives from wet-weather or outlier laps; likely needs separate handling for slick vs wet compounds.
